creator: koobs@FreeBSD.org
text:
Currently the AutoAssigner adds certain Keywords: based on the presence of [tags] within the issue Summary:

Much of what the AutoAssiger does also prevents changes to the organisational schema/structure quickly/easily within the Bugzilla via frontend user interface, given it relies heavily on fields names and values. Examples of this include:

- Product/Component names
- Flag names/values
- Summary contents / Keywords

One example of this is to add the keyword 'patch' when the string "[patch]" is present in the Summary.

The initial motivation for this was:

- To make issues with patches easy to find/work on for developers
- To reduce triage time (adding the patch keyword if there was an attachment)

After having evaluated its value/impact since it was deployed:

- Duplicates what is a pre-existing and 'intrinsic' issue/bug attribute (issue has an attachment of type patch)
- Creates a second (fragmented) and inaccurate method to identify issues/bugs with patches, given keywords are manually added, or only conditionally added in the presence of a manually added Summary string.
- Is not always true/accurate (patches get deleted/obsoleted)
- Relies on manual input, and knowledge of the mechanism
- Prevents keywords from being removed/renamed (autoassigner may not behave/degrade gracefully if an expected field is no longer present)

Going forward it is desirable to minimise the amount of 'additive' metadata that needs to be manually added to triage/classify/accurately describe an issue, versus relying on existing or intrinsic attributes of an issue/bug.

Therefore this functionality is to be removed, which will unblock other classification/schema improvements being made (in this case and in particular, for Keywords:)
